BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Standby projection for Reyes Renovations, LLC — a possible weekend home-renovation side operation run out of the Ironbound. Numbers are planning estimates only; Lisa is still W-2 full-time at Turner. Unit = one typical bathroom gut-and-tile job (demo, drywall, tile, grout, punch-list).

CurrentState.Note: Assumes one bathroom job per month plus small repairs; weekends only, no impact on Turner schedule.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: One bathroom job per month
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed overhead ~$834 / job margin $2,670 per bathroom = 0.31 jobs to cover fixed costs; one job per month clears roughly $1,800.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Covers fixed overhead and leaves a modest owner draw; sustainable as a true side gig.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Two bathroom jobs per month
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Roughly doubles net but needs more of Hector's weekend hours and a second tile saw.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Fixed monthly overhead (insurance, storage, truck) runs about $834. After materials and crew help on a typical $4,600 bathroom job, the side operation clears roughly $1,800/month at one job per month. Two jobs in a month is where it actually starts being worth the weekends.
ActionItems: Confirm general liability quote from Garden State Insurance before taking on first paid job
Lock a standard demo + tile bathroom package price at $4,200 to simplify estimating
Hold off buying a second tile saw until booking 3 confirmed weekend jobs
